items.situation: third-floor study lounge closing for ceiling pipe repair
items.announcement: Attention students. The third-floor study lounge in the Bradley Library will be closed all next week, beginning this Monday, so that maintenance crews can repair a leaking ceiling pipe. We're sorry for the disruption, but the work can't be delayed any longer. During the closure, students may use the quiet study rooms on the first floor, which will stay open until midnight each night. Please remove any personal belongings from the lounge lockers before Friday at 5 p.m., since the lockers will be emptied during the repairs. We expect the lounge to reopen the following Monday. Thanks for your patience.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this announcement?

options.A: To explain a temporary closure of a study lounge
options.B: To introduce new midnight library hours
options.C: To encourage students to use the lockers more
options.D: To advertise a newly renovated reading room

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：通知的核心是图书馆三楼自习室因维修水管而临时关闭，并说明替代方案，A准确概括了整体目的。B错（too_narrow）：一楼安静自习室开到午夜只是临时替代细节，不是通知的主要目的。C错（reversed_intent）：通知实际上要求学生在周五前清空储物柜，而非鼓励多用储物柜，意思相反。D错（wrong_purpose）：通知里没有任何新装修阅览室的内容，这是一个貌似合理但与本通知无关的目的。

questions.stem: What must students do before Friday at 5 p.m.?

options.A: Reserve a first-floor quiet study room
options.B: Report leaking pipes to maintenance staff
options.C: Remove their belongings from the lounge lockers
options.D: Return library books to the third floor

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：通知明确要求学生在周五下午五点前从自习室储物柜取走个人物品，因为维修期间储物柜会被清空。A错（related_but_not_stated）：一楼自习室是替代场所，但通知没有要求预约。B错（misattributed_detail）：漏水的管道是维修人员要修的对象，并非要学生去报告。D错（related_but_not_stated）：通知完全没有提到还书。

items.situation: grand reopening of a renovated campus performance hall
items.announcement: Good afternoon, everyone. We're excited to announce that the newly renovated Harmon Performance Hall will officially reopen this Saturday at 7 p.m. with a free concert by the university orchestra. The renovation added improved acoustic panels, wider seating, and a fully accessible side entrance off Lincoln Street. Doors will open at 6:15 p.m., and seating is first-come, first-served, so we recommend arriving early. Although the concert is free, you'll need to pick up a ticket in advance from the box office in the student center. Light refreshments will be served in the lobby after the performance. We hope to see you there.

questions.stem: What is the announcement mainly about?

options.A: Construction delays at the performance hall
options.B: The reopening of a renovated performance hall
options.D: A fundraiser to repair the concert hall

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：通知核心是装修一新的Harmon演出厅本周六重新开放并举办免费音乐会，B准确概括。A错（reversed_intent）：通知宣布的是顺利重开，而非施工延误，意思相反。C错（wrong_purpose）：完全没有提到乐团选拔。D错（wrong_purpose）：音乐会是免费的，没有任何募捐修缮内容。

questions.stem: What must attendees do before the concert?

options.A: Pay an admission fee at the door
options.C: Enter through the Lincoln Street entrance
options.D: Pick up a free ticket from the box office

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：通知说明音乐会虽免费，但需提前到学生中心售票处领取门票。A错（wrong_detail）：音乐会是免费的，门口不收费，与事实相反。B错（wrong_detail）：座位是先到先得，并非网上预订对号入座。C错（misattributed_detail）：Lincoln街入口是新增的无障碍侧门细节，并非所有人入场的必须步骤。

items.situation: international club cultural food festival on the main green
items.announcement: Hi everyone, this is the International Students Club. We're thrilled to invite you to our annual Taste of the World food festival this Thursday from noon to 4 p.m. on the main green. Members from more than twenty countries will be serving homemade dishes, and everything is included with a single five-dollar wristband sold at the entrance tent. All proceeds this year will go toward a scholarship fund for incoming international students. Since dishes tend to run out quickly, we'd suggest coming closer to noon rather than later in the afternoon. Wristbands are cash only, so please plan ahead. We can't wait to share our food with you.

questions.stem: How can students get food at the festival?

options.A: By signing up on the club's website
options.B: By bringing a dish from their own country
options.C: By buying a five-dollar wristband at the entrance
options.D: By showing a valid student ID at the tent

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：通知说明在入口帐篷购买五美元手环即可享用全部菜品。A错（related_but_not_stated）：网上报名是常见校园做法，但本通知没有提及。B错（wrong_detail）：自带家乡菜的是来自各国的社团成员（供应方），普通学生不需要带菜。D错（related_but_not_stated）：出示学生证貌似合理，但通知里入场凭的是手环而非学生证。

questions.stem: Why does the speaker recommend arriving closer to noon?

options.A: Because popular dishes may sell out later
options.B: Because wristband prices rise in the afternoon
options.C: Because the festival ends earlier than planned
options.D: Because parking near the green is limited at noon

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：通知说菜品往往很快售罄，因此建议早些来——可推断出晚来可能买不到想吃的菜。B错（over_inference）：通知从未说手环下午涨价，属过度推断。C错（wrong_detail）：活动到下午四点结束，没有提前结束。D错（unrelated_inference）：停车位紧张是合理推断，但通知中毫无相关证据，属于无关推断。

items.situation: interdisciplinary student society conference call for papers
items.announcement: I'm pleased to announce that the Graduate Interdisciplinary Society is now accepting paper proposals for our spring conference, titled Crossing Boundaries. We welcome work that connects two or more fields, whether that's linking biology with ethics or economics with art history. Proposals should be a single page describing your topic and methods, and they must be submitted through the society's online portal by March 20th. Please note that you don't need a finished paper to apply; we're looking for promising ideas at any stage. Selected presenters will give fifteen-minute talks in Hartwell Hall on April 18th. If you have questions, stop by our office in room 210.

questions.stem: What is required to submit a proposal?

options.A: A completed and polished research paper
options.D: A one-page description of the topic and methods

questions.explanation: 正确答案D：通知明确要求提交一页纸说明选题和方法。A错（wrong_detail）：通知特别指出不需要完成的论文，与之相反。B错（related_but_not_stated）：导师推荐信是常见学术要求，但本通知没有提到。C错（misattributed_detail）：通知说的是研究要跨两个或更多领域，而非要求申请者属于两个院系，把内容张冠李戴了。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about who may apply to the conference?

options.B: Students whose work bridges different fields
options.C: Students who have already finished their thesis
options.D: Only graduate students in the sciences

questions.explanation: 正确答案B：会议名为Crossing Boundaries，欢迎连接两个或更多领域的研究（如生物与伦理），可推断适合做跨学科研究的学生。A错（over_inference）：通知欢迎任何阶段的有潜力想法，并未要求已发表研究，属过度推断。C错（over_inference）：通知明确说无需完成论文，更不必完成学位论文。D错（wrong_detail）：例子里包含艺术史、经济学等，并不限于理科，属错误细节。

items.situation: study abroad information session featuring returned students
items.announcement: This is a reminder that the Office of Global Programs will host its study abroad information session this Wednesday at 4 p.m. in Carver Hall, room 105. Rather than a slideshow from staff, this session features four students who recently returned from semesters in Japan, Chile, Ghana, and France. They'll speak candidly about budgeting, coursework that transferred, and challenges they didn't expect. We'll leave plenty of time for your questions afterward. While walk-ins are welcome, we ask that you reserve a seat on the Global Programs website so we can prepare enough handouts. Bring a notebook; the returning students always share more practical tips than any brochure could.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this announcement?

options.A: To promote an information session led by returned students
options.B: To announce new study abroad destinations for next year
options.C: To collect applications for a study abroad scholarship
options.D: To recruit students to study specifically in Japan

questions.explanation: 正确答案A：通知核心是宣传周三由四名刚归国学生主讲的留学信息说明会，A准确概括整体目的。B错（wrong_purpose）：日本、智利等只是这些学生去过的地方，而非宣布新增目的地。C错（wrong_purpose）：通知里没有任何奖学金申请内容。D错（too_narrow）：日本只是四个国家之一，把目的窄化为只推荐去日本是片面的。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about why returning students lead the session?

options.A: Staff members are unavailable on Wednesday
options.B: The office wants to reduce printing costs
options.C: Their firsthand experience is seen as more useful
options.D: The session counts as academic course credit

questions.explanation: 正确答案C：通知特意说不用员工放幻灯片，而由归国学生分享，并强调他们给的实用建议比任何宣传册都多——可推断学校认为亲历者经验更有价值。A错（over_inference）：通知没说员工没空，属凭空推断。B错（unrelated_inference）：预约是为了准备足够讲义，与降低印刷成本无关。D错（over_inference）：通知完全没提学分，属过度推断。
